text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Whiskersdale lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver had a passion for history—he knew every tree was named for a different historical figure—and had quite the collection of old-books on just about every branch.

One autumn morning, as the leaves goldened, Oliver discovered a parched scrapbook tucked between the branches of his favourite oak tree. Inside lay a dear old photograph album belonging to a boy who had lived mere years ago, a boy named Ethan.

The memories in Ethan's photographs were like little windows into a different time—a summersault of laughter, a first-time birdwatching with grandma, a school talent show victory dance. Among them was especially dear—a photo of Ethan with a tiny telescope perched on his shoulder, peering into the night sky.

Oliver felt a warmth spread through him. Ethan might have lived long ago, but his spirit was still alive somewhere in the world. That evening, Oliver shared the stories behind each
